Here's the robot observation at the end of the task execution:
pot (empty and clean), third stove burner (turned off), fourth stove burner (turned on), second stove burner (turned off), first stove burner (turned off). pot is on top of fourth stove burner. pot is on the left of stove knob. pot is on the right of salt shaker. pot is on the right of pepper shaker. pot is on the right of spatula. nothing is inside robot gripper.
The robot plan is:
00:18. Goal: Pick up pot.
00:25. Goal: Put pot in sink.
00:28. Goal: Pick up pot.
00:42. Goal: Put pot on fourth stove burner.
00:45. Goal: Toggle on fourth stove burner.

Q: Known that all actions in the robot plan were executed successfully, what's wrong with the robot plan that caused the robot to fail?
A:

response: The plan never included filling the pot with water. Although the pot ended up on an active burner, it was still empty, so the goal condition for boiling water was not met.
